compiled_state.title: Add Pattern Reference subsection to implementation-plan SKILL.md Step 3
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Story
90e1-a0e4 — Enriched task descriptions: Pattern Reference section

## Goal
Add a new `### Pattern Reference` subsection to Step 3 (Task Drafting) of plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md. This subsection instructs agents to conditionally include a `## Pattern Reference` section in each generated task description, containing ≤30 lines of codebase examples when pattern familiarity (from complexity evaluator Dimension 7) is low or medium.

## Implementation
Add the subsection after `### Consumer Detection Pass` in Step 3 with the following behavior:
- If complexity evaluator output includes `pattern_familiarity: low` or `pattern_familiarity: medium` → run grep/glob on codebase to retrieve ≤30 lines of representative examples → include as `## Pattern Reference` section in the task description
- If `pattern_familiarity: high` OR no evaluator output → omit the section entirely
- Pattern retrieval uses only grep/glob on local codebase (no external calls, no LLM calls)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `### Pattern Reference` heading exists in Step 3 (test_pattern_reference_heading_present passes)
2. Section describes inclusion logic for low/medium familiarity (test_pattern_reference_low_familiarity_rule passes)
3. Section mentions ≤30-line cap (test_pattern_reference_line_cap_rule passes)
4. Structural boundary tests in tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-pattern-reference.sh all GREEN

## Files to Modify
- plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md (UPDATE — add ### Pattern Reference subsection in Step 3)

## Dependencies
Depends on 91fa-a8f5 (RED test must exist before implementation)
